title: Ipomoea abyssinica
intro: Ipomoea abyssinica is a flowering plant species belonging to the morning glory family (Convolvulaceae), native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its purported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ipomoea abyssinica include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, or used as a paste or ointment for topical application, making it a popular ingredient in various herbal remedies.
taxonomy: Ipomoea abyssinica is classified within the family Convolvulaceae, commonly known as the morning glory family. This family comprises around 1,800 species of flowering plants. Convolvulaceae is a part of the larger order Solanales, which includes several other families of flowering plants. The family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Ipomoea abyssinica is a species of flowering plant within the Convolvulaceae family, native to Ethiopia.

In traditional Ethiopian medicine, it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and roots are often used to make infusions or decoctions for therapeutic purposes.
